summary: Replace rejected polygon-first map drawing and spatial index with terrain-route-node-first rendering and interaction
acceptance_criteria: Replace or bypass the rejected current map drawing direction and polygon-first spatial index where needed; do not treat this as visual polish over the old topology-backed polygon map.

Default player map renders terrain patches/bands, barriers/channels, strategic nodes, route corridors, and governance footprints as distinct layers, with governance as overlay rather than primary board cells.
Hit testing prioritizes route corridors, strategic nodes, crossings/ports/passes/blockers, and terrain explanation layers before district governance footprints.
Route previews are drawn from accepted terrain-route-node query results, not from polygon centroids, bounding boxes, nearest-neighbor geometry, hidden grid/lattice ids, or renderer-only fake lines.
Player-facing route preview shows node/corridor sequence, mode, estimated days, bottleneck capacity, season state, terrain reason, blocker state, risk, and governance consequences when relevant.
The map/task rail/district list/inspector stay synchronized when the active object is a strategic node, route corridor, terrain feature, blocker, or governance overlay.
The UI remains low-fidelity and does not introduce production art, final GIS assets, production cultural symbols, or manual node battle UI.
required_tests: Playwright screenshots at 1440x900 and 1280x720 proving the map reads as strategic terrain rather than a disguised cell board
Playwright or component tests proving node/corridor hit priority over governance overlays and route preview reason parity with query output
notes: This task is intentionally allowed to replace the old renderer/spatial-index direction inside its allowed paths after schema and fixture acceptance.
Protocol/schema/content changes belong to earlier tasks; this task consumes accepted read/query surfaces.
Default player text should explain decisions, not implementation terms or debug ids.
As of RR0 roadmap adoption, this task is non-active retained evidence and must not be treated as dependency-ready implementation work.
Partial diff and handoff evidence remain preserved in the original M7 renderer worktree; RR0 does not copy product code from that worktree.
next_action: Start renderer-interaction work after M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-FIXTURE-001 closed in main.

non_active_retention.reason: Accepted product recovery roadmap resets forward maturity to R0; this M7 renderer task must not remain operational active work.
non_active_retention.next_review_path: RR0-REPOSITORY-STATE-RECONCILIATION-001 decides whether to split, abandon, or migrate preserved evidence.
